entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: A country in West Africa where rainfall patterns and their impact on agriculture were studied

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: The long-term shift in weather patterns, specifically the decline in rainfall in Niger

entities.name: ANNUAL RAINFALL
entities.description: The total yearly precipitation, which showed a decline after 1960 in Niger

entities.name: MOVING AVERAGES
entities.description: A statistical technique used to smooth out short-term fluctuations and highlight longer-term trends in annual rainfall

entities.name: CORRELATION COEFFICIENTS
entities.description: A statistical measure used to determine the strength of the relationship between monthly and annual rainfall

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The period of the year characterized by rainfall, with parameters like onset, ending, and number of rainy days

entities.name: GROWING SEASON
entities.description: The period during which crops can grow, which was reduced by 5-20 days in Niger

entities.name: WATER BALANCE CALCULATIONS
entities.description: A method used to determine the probability of rainfall exceeding potential evapotranspiration

entities.name: POTENTIAL EVAPOTRANSPIRATION
entities.description: The amount of water that would evaporate and transpire from a surface under specific environmental conditions

entities.name: AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The practice of farming in Niger, which is impacted by changes in rainfall and growing season

entities.name: CROPPING
entities.description: The act of growing crops, which has become more risky due to increased variability in rain onset and ending
